text: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are.
author: Carl Jung

text: When we treat man as he is, we make him worse than he is; when we treat him as if he already were what he potentially could be, we make him what he should be.
author: Carl Rogers

text: Everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms—to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: The good life is a process, not a state of being. It is a direction not a destination.
author: Carl Rogers

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: To love is to be vulnerable.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: The only way to deal with an unfree world is to become so absolutely free that your very existence is an act of rebellion.
author: Albert Camus

text: Man is condemned to be free; because once thrown into the world, he is responsible for everything he does.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: You are not a drop in the ocean. You are the entire ocean in a drop.
author: Rumi

text: The curious paradox is that when I accept myself just as I am, then I can change.
author: Carl Rogers

text: Knowing your own darkness is the best method for dealing with the darknesses of other people.
author: Carl Jung

text: Freedom from desire leads to inner peace.
author: Lao Tzu

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: It is not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
author: Epictetus

text: The greatest revolution of our generation is the discovery that human beings, by changing the inner attitudes of their minds, can change the outer aspects of their lives.
author: William James

text: Loneliness is and always has been the central and inevitable experience of every man.
author: Thomas Wolfe

text: Perhaps all the dragons in our lives are princesses who are only waiting to see us act, just once, with beauty and courage.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: The most terrifying thing is to accept oneself completely.
author: Carl Jung

text: Life is not a problem to be solved, but a reality to be experienced.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

text: Your vision will become clear only when you can look into your own heart.
author: Carl Jung

text: No tree, it is said, can grow to heaven unless its roots reach down to hell.
author: Carl Jung

text: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: What you deny, you submit to. What you accept, transforms you.
author: Carl Jung

text: Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: Those who do not look within themselves never look anywhere else.
author: Michel de Montaigne

text: True strength is keeping everything together when everyone expects you to fall apart.
author: Unknown

text: Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.
author: Buddha

text: The first step to healing is becoming aware of the wound.
author: Unknown

text: Anxiety is love's greatest killer.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: We are not human beings having a spiritual experience. We are spiritual beings having a human experience.
author: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin

text: The shoe that fits one person pinches another; there is no recipe for living that suits all cases.
author: Carl Jung

text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.
author: Marie Curie

text: The ability to be in the present moment is a major component of mental wellness.
author: Abraham Maslow

text: Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most.
author: Aristotle

text: Growth begins when we begin to accept our own weakness.
author: Jean Vanier

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Realize deeply that the present moment is all you have.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Comparison is the thief of joy.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: You cannot swim for new horizons until you have courage to lose sight of the shore.
author: William Faulkner

text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

text: Be gentle with yourself. You are doing the best you can.
author: Unknown

text: Perfectionism is the voice of the oppressor.
author: Anne Lamott

text: Rest is not idleness, and to lie sometimes on the grass under trees on a summer's day... is by no means a waste of time.
author: John Lubbock

text: Sometimes the bravest thing you can do is ask for help.
author: Unknown

text: Your task is not to seek for love, but merely to seek and find all the barriers within yourself that you have built against it.
author: Rumi

text: The privilege of a lifetime is being who you are.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: Do not let the behavior of others destroy your inner peace.
author: Dalai Lama

text: Accept yourself, love yourself, and keep moving forward.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: Silence is not an absence but a presence.
author: Anne D. LeClaire

text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.
author: Ram Dass

text: Patience is not the ability to wait, but the ability to keep a good attitude while waiting.
author: Joyce Meyer

text: Let go or be dragged.
author: Zen Proverb

text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Courage doesn't always roar. Sometimes courage is the quiet voice at the end of the day saying, 'I will try again tomorrow'.
author: Mary Anne Radmacher

text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
author: Dalai Lama

text: The best way out is always through.
author: Robert Frost

text: One looks back with appreciation to the brilliant teachers, but with gratitude to those who touched our human feelings.
author: Carl Jung
